title: Sree Shivashakthi Mahaganapathi temple
tagline: Sree Shivashakthi Mahaganapathi temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Sree Shivashakthi Mahaganapathi temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Shiva, Devi Parvati and Lord Ganapathi.
overview.description: Sree Shivashakthi Mahaganapathi temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Shiva, Devi Parvati and Lord Ganapathi.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Chenkal
topPlaces.description: Chenkal is a village in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India. Until the land-reforms ordinance enacted by the Communist regime in the 1950s, the village formed part of the estate of the jenmi (Yejamanan) of Kandamath.

topPlaces.name: Arayoor Sree Mahadevar
topPlaces.description: Arayoor Major Sree Mahadevar Temple (ആറയൂർ ശ്രീ മഹാദേവർ) is one of the major worship places in Arayoor in Thiruvananthapuram district, Kerala, very close to Tamil Nadu border. It is a Shiva temple.

topPlaces.name: Kulathoor, Thiruvananthapuram
topPlaces.description: Kulathoor, is a village in Neyyattinkara Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Thirupuram
topPlaces.description: Thirupuram is a village in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India. Thirupuram village is 6 km away from Neyyattinkara, 25 km away from the capital city Thiruvananthapuram.

topPlaces.name: Arumanoor
topPlaces.description: Arumanoor is a village on the banks of the river Meenachil in Kottayam district, Kerala, India. It is between the towns of Ettumanoor and Ayarkunnam, and has a population of 2,000.

topPlaces.name: Parasuvaikkal
topPlaces.description: Parasuvaikkal is a village in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India. Parasuvaikkal has four Hindu temples, Parasuvaikkal major Sree Bhagavathy temple governed by Travancore Devaswom Board, Ponnamkulam Devi Kshethram, Idanatukonam Sree Dharma Sastha Temple and Kottakkakam Sree Mahadeva Kshethram Trust.

topPlaces.name: Karode
topPlaces.description: Karode is a village in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Kottamom
topPlaces.description: Kottamom is a village in the Neyyattinkara Taluk which is part of the Thiruvananthapuram district of Kerala, a state in India Kottamom comes under Chenkal Panchayat and comes under the South Kerala Division. Kottamom is located 4 km from Parassala, 5.5 km from Neyyattinkara, and 30 km from Thiruvananthapuram.

items.note: Chenkal is a village in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India. Until the land-reforms ordinance enacted by the Communist regime in the 1950s, the village formed part of the estate of the jenmi (Yejamanan) of Kandamath.
Arayoor Major Sree Mahadevar Temple (ആറയൂർ ശ്രീ മഹാദേവർ) is one of the major worship places in Arayoor in Thiruvananthapuram district, Kerala, very close to Tamil Nadu border. It is a Shiva temple.
Kulathoor, is a village in Neyyattinkara Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India.
Thirupuram is a village in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India. Thirupuram village is 6 km away from Neyyattinkara, 25 km away from the capital city Thiruvananthapuram.
Arumanoor is a village on the banks of the river Meenachil in Kottayam district, Kerala, India. It is between the towns of Ettumanoor and Ayarkunnam, and has a population of 2,000.

Parasuvaikkal is a village in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India. Parasuvaikkal has four Hindu temples, Parasuvaikkal major Sree Bhagavathy temple governed by Travancore Devaswom Board, Ponnamkulam Devi Kshethram, Idanatukonam Sree Dharma Sastha Temple and Kottakkakam Sree Mahadeva Kshethram Trust.
Karode is a village in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India.

Kottamom is a village in the Neyyattinkara Taluk which is part of the Thiruvananthapuram district of Kerala, a state in India Kottamom comes under Chenkal Panchayat and comes under the South Kerala Division. Kottamom is located 4 km from Parassala, 5.5 km from Neyyattinkara, and 30 km from Thiruvananthapuram.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sree Shivashakthi Mahaganapathi temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sree Shivashakthi Mahaganapathi temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sree Shivashakthi Mahaganapathi temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (25 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (23 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sree Shivashakthi Mahaganapathi temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sree Shivashakthi Mahaganapathi temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sree Shivashakthi Mahaganapathi temple?
faq.a: Stays in Sree Shivashakthi Mahaganapathi temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sree Shivashakthi Mahaganapathi temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Chenkal, Arayoor Sree Mahadevar, Kulathoor, Thiruvananthapuram, Thirupuram, Arumanoor.
